summary: Deterministic proof that `cautilus evaluate comparison prepare` emits machine-readable baseline and candidate paths plus downstream command arrays that thread the candidate path into `evaluate fixture` and `evaluate review variants`.

evidenceScope.claimBoundary: Only the docs/guides/evaluation-process.md claim about machine-readable baseline/candidate paths and downstream command reuse is marked satisfied.
notClaimed: Every operator will actually pass the emitted paths into follow-up commands.
Every candidate workspace is semantically safe to evaluate.
Review variants are unable to mutate a candidate when a consumer-owned command does so.
The helper validates external adapter quality.

protectedBehaviors: `evaluate comparison prepare` writes JSON with `baseline.path` and `candidate.path` fields.
The emitted baseline and candidate paths point at worktrees containing the expected checked-out files.
The emitted `usage.evaluateFixture` command array includes `cautilus evaluate fixture --repo-root <repo> --workspace <candidate.path>`.
The emitted `usage.reviewVariants` command array includes `cautilus evaluate review variants --repo-root <repo> --workspace <candidate.path>`.

decision.reason: The focused Go smoke test executes the public CLI command, parses stdout as JSON, verifies baseline.path and candidate.path are machine-readable strings pointing at materialized worktrees, and verifies the candidate path is threaded into both downstream command arrays named by the guide.
